items.title: Buyer Discovery Relevance: operating discovery
items.name: Buyer Discovery Relevance: operating discovery
items.summary: Discovery work is testing whether buyer discovery relevance can move the linked customer and business KPIs while addressing liquidity and trust while classifieds mix shifts toward professional sellers and services.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Buyer Discovery Relevance Initiative is the quarter's focused investment to move Successful buyer transaction rate and Demand-side liquidity contribution, anchored in liquidity and trust while classifieds mix shifts toward professional sellers and services and led through Improve category-aware ranking, filtering, and local-intent relevance.. The quarter concentrates on trust, liquidity, and professional-seller quality in local marketplace flows so the work reads like a realistic OKR-driven investment set for a company with roughly 250 product and technology people.

assignments.how: Leads the discovery framing, evidence synthesis, and recommendation on how far this initiative should scale.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.
Adds workflow, commercial, and implementation evidence needed to close the main delivery risks.

riskFocus.question: Will customers buy the product and/or choose to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Customers will engage more consistently if buyer discovery relevance measurably improves successful buyer transaction rate.
evidenceToCollect: Whether target users describe successful buyer transaction rate as a top-priority pain point.
Whether the proposed workflow changes are strong enough to change behavior, not just preference.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The investment is worth scaling when it improves demand-side liquidity contribution without creating disproportionate operating cost or support load.
evidenceToCollect: Which economic or operating constraints cap the upside.
What minimum KPI movement would justify continued investment.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Users can complete the redesigned workflow with less guidance and less back-and-forth than the current path.
evidenceToCollect: Where users hesitate in the new flow.
Which messages, states, or transitions remain unclear.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can deliver the smallest useful slice inside current architecture and staffing constraints without creating a long tail of one-off work.
evidenceToCollect: What can be standardized versus localized or custom.

plannedActivities: Review drop-off points in listing creation, trust verification, and first-response journeys.
Compare outcome quality between casual sellers, power users, and professional merchants.
Test whether stronger trust cues improve reply quality without reducing listing volume.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to push harder on trust controls now or sequence them behind supply and response-rate improvements.

items.opportunity: Clarify whether this bet is strong enough to scale in the next planning cycle, or whether the organization should narrow scope and protect focus.

linkedInitiatives.description: Casual Seller Listing Quality Initiative is the quarter's focused investment to move Listing-to-sale realization and Supply liquidity and monetization, anchored in liquidity and trust while classifieds mix shifts toward professional sellers and services and led through Add category-aware listing templates and better draft recovery..
